title: Who is Lilith and who is Eve for each Adam
display_title: Who is Lilith and who is Eve for each Adam
wikitext:
[]: Who is Lilith and who is Eve for each Adam?             Quote Post by Barinax » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:09 pm Ok. We know that there is angel Adam... but at the same time, others are also considered Adam... sometimes metaphorically. Adam Gendo Kaworu Shinji Would any of these not be considered Adam, or are there any more that could be considered Adam? And in each case, who is the Eve and the Lilith? This is probably a silly question.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:13 pm Barinax wrote: This is probably a silly question.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:16 pm Gendo has relation to Adam as he grafted Adam's devolved embryo body onto the palm of his hand. Kaworu has relation to Adam in that he's what happened when they salvaged Adam's soul. In other words, they probably cloned him some poor sod's human body and stuck Adam's soul in it, like the Lilith soul and Yui body dealie. Shinji has no relation to Adam whatsoever. Eve is not referenced in Evangelion whatsoever. Lilith is that big ole white giant on the cross. It's soul is currently residing in Yui Ikari's cloned body right now, acting as Rei. It's not really silly, just what's the point? Keep in mind, the religious imagery is mostly pedantics anyway, and it just there for the cool aesthetic, because like no one in Japan is Christian. If they knew it was gonna be a cultural phenomenon, they said they wouldn't have done it. Yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:18 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Eve is not referenced in Evangelion whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:19 pm ...huh. I never knew that. I just took it as the shortened form of Evangelion, which is what it is. A clever ruse, Anno!

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:47 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Barinax wrote: This is probably a silly question.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:52 pm Symbolism that means nothing to the narative. Seriously, there is no Christian meaning to the show so looking for it is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Oct 09, 2007 7:56 pm Shinji is the only one of those who really has nothing to do with Adam (assuming the Spanish website wasn't telling the truth).

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Tue Oct 09, 2007 8:42 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Symbolism that means nothing to the narative. Seriously, there is no Christian meaning to the show so looking for it is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Oct 09, 2007 9:03 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Seriously, there is no Christian meaning to the show so looking for it is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Oct 09, 2007 9:07 pm Oh my god, Jimbo is back! Run away, run away! Just kidding, buddy, you know I love you.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Oct 09, 2007 9:20 pm BrikHaus wrote: Oh my god, Jimbo is back! Run away, run away! Just kidding, buddy, you know I love you.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Oct 09, 2007 9:41 pm Oh Jimbo, how this place has not been the same without you. Sorry for being off topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Oct 09, 2007 9:50 pm hehe, thanks Trigger. "You like me. You really really like me!" I'll do it: "Back on topic, bitches!"

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Oct 09, 2007 10:08 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Is admitting one's own pedantry being an ass or is there something to the quote that I missed?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Oct 09, 2007 10:12 pm Yeah, he yelled "TOO BAD!" into the microphone in English, then prattled on about he was a tree wizard. Needless to say, several audience members became quite disillusioned with Anno's work after that fateful panel.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Oct 09, 2007 10:23 pm Ahhhh, where would art be without the loopy, angry geniuses behind it? Beethoven was a notorious arsehole too.* * I'm not comparing Anno to Beethoven or saying he's on the same level as the true geniuses in art, but he does strike me as the classic "few whiskers short of a Hello Kitty Doll" artist.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Oct 09, 2007 11:42 pm Yeah, he yelled "TOO BAD!" into the microphone in English, then prattled on about he was a tree wizard. Needless to say, several audience members became quite disillusioned with Anno's work after that fateful panel.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Oct 10, 2007 12:08 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Ahhhh, where would art be without the loopy, angry geniuses behind it?

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Wed Oct 10, 2007 10:29 am Eva Yojimbo"][quote="Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Seriously, there is no Christian meaning to the show so looking for it is pointless.
